challenges.title: Iterator Make
challenges.description: Create function fibIterator(limit) that returns an iterable yielding Fibonacci numbers up to limit. Test with limit 10. Expected output: `1,1,2,3,5,8`.
challenges.hint: Return an object with [Symbol.iterator]() that returns a next() method following the iterator protocol.

steps.title: Understand the goal
steps.explanation: We need a custom iterable that produces Fibonacci numbers up to a given limit.

steps.title: Implement the solution
steps.explanation: Implement the iterable protocol with [Symbol.iterator]() returning an object with next(). Track two variables for the Fibonacci sequence.

steps.title: Verify the result
steps.explanation: Fibonacci numbers up to 10 are 1,1,2,3,5,8, matching the expected output.

challenges.title: DFS Path
challenges.description: Write function dfsPath(graph, start, end) that finds a path between two nodes using depth-first search. Test with {A:["B","C"], B:["D"], C:[], D:["E"], E:[]} from "A" to "E". Expected output: `A,B,D,E`.
challenges.hint: Use recursive DFS with a visited set. Build the path as you go and return it when the end node is found.

steps.title: Understand the goal
steps.explanation: We need to find a path in a graph from start to end using depth-first search.

steps.title: Implement the solution
steps.explanation: Recursively visit neighbors, building the path. When the end node is reached, return the path. Backtrack on dead ends.

steps.title: Verify the result
steps.explanation: DFS finds the path A -> B -> D -> E, printing A,B,D,E.
